title: China restricts civilian drone exports, citing Ukraine and concern about military use
bodyHtml:
In a letter received from the Ministry of Trade, it has been stated that the Ministry of Commerce of China introduced certain regulations and restrictions on the export of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) due to security concerns on July 31, 2023. This situation could potentially impact the companies in our country engaged in the production of civilian UAVs. Accordingly, it is advised that these companies take necessary precautions before September 1, 2023.

On Monday, July 31, 2023, the Ministry of Commerce of China announced that, in order to safeguard China's national security and interests, certain items related to unmanned aerial vehicles will be subjected to export controls starting from September 1, 2023. This decision is in accordance with the relevant provisions of the "Export Control Law of the People's Republic of China," "Foreign Trade Law of the People's Republic of China," and "Customs Law of the People's Republic of China."

According to the announced regulations, the following products will not be allowed for unauthorized export starting from September 1:

• Air engines with a maximum continuous power exceeding 16 kilowatts (kW) designated for unmanned aerial vehicles. Relevant HS codes (see 8501530010, 8407101010, 8407102010, 8408909230, 8408909320, 8411111010, 84111119010, 8411121010, 8411129020, 8411210010, 8411221010, 8411222010, 8411223010, 8411810002)

• Payloads specifically used for unmanned aerial vehicles that meet certain technical indicators, including infrared imaging equipment, synthetic aperture radar, and lasers for target identification. (HS Codes: 8525891110, 8525892110, 8525893110)

• Radio communication equipment with specific features allocated for certain UAVs or unmanned aerial ships that meet any of the following specifications. (Relevant HS codes: 8517629910, 8517691002, 8526920010)

• Anti-Unmanned Aerial Vehicle Equipment: (Relevant HS codes: 8543709960, 9013200093)

Under the listed conditions, companies seeking to export unmanned aerial vehicles must apply to the Ministry of Trade in order to ensure compliance.
